checklist.claim: the rc-2 stderr discriminator cannot false-positive a clean scan for this caller (explicit --marker short-circuits the config read)
checklist.evidence: reproduced: with --marker, clean scan -> rc2 + empty stderr; without --marker + malformed config -> rc2 + stderr. Helper always passes --marker.

phase3_findings.description: The missing/unreadable-sibling tests assert only the outcome, which either of the helper's two independent screens satisfies alone; the suite stayed GREEN when either guard was deleted individually. The precheck is the robust half (the discriminator no-ops without $WP_ERR and relies on explicit --marker), so a refactor deleting it would ship green.
The unreadable-sibling SKIP breadcrumb prints to stdout but is not counted in the PASS/FAIL tally, so under a mode-bit-ignoring uid two assertions vanish from the count.
no findings; all newest comment claims verified true against HEAD
no findings; discriminator false-positive, WP_ERR reuse, flip_to_failed stderr, merged-main interactions all cleared

fix_decisions.claim_text: sibling-guard tests are under-constrained; either guard is individually deletable with the suite green
fix_decisions.evidence: Shape 3 flagged (single-source from pr-test-analyzer, an empirical over-grader). Re-affirmed at Important, and independently mutation-verified by the orchestrator BEFORE the agent reported it: variants removing each guard alone both stayed GREEN. The test fails OPEN in the direction that matters — a plausible one-line refactor deleting the robust precheck ships green while regressing the partial-vendor-copy deployment shape. Same class, same grade, as the iteration-1 ENGINE_ERROR vacuous-pin finding; grading it lower purely because its blast radius is latent would be inconsistent. Not diagnostic-only; the suite does not catch the regression RED.

fix_decisions.claim_text: SKIP breadcrumb not counted in the PASS/FAIL tally
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion severity. Matches the repo's established pattern (a comparable `SKIP #268` exists), the suite has no expected-total invariant, and the non-uid-gated missing-sibling assertions still cover the class. The breadcrumb already makes the skip visible in the log.
